title: [Krookodile] Battle Guide
summary: A [Ground]/[Dark] snowball [sweeper] that stacks [moxie] boosts off every KO and rolls through weakened teams.
strengths: [moxie] grants a +1 Atk stack on every KO — free snowball [sweeper]
[earthquake] and [knock-off] STAB combo has almost no safe answers
92 Speed outruns most bulky attackers in the tier
weaknesses: [Fairy] walls completely stall the STAB combination
12.5 percent [stealth-rock] chip eats into the snowball runway

recommendedMoves.reason: Core [Ground] STAB and the main engine for snowball kills
[Dark] STAB plus item removal in a [sing]le high-value c[lick]
Handles [Steel] and [Normal] walls while stacking [moxie] faster

recommendedItems.reason: Guarantees the first KO so can start the snowball
Pushes 92 Speed into top-tier cove[rage] without [tailwind] help
A flat 20 percent boost to for sand-leaning builds

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Krookodile]'s base 117/92 line is solid on its own, but the whole sell is [moxie] — a +1 Atk stack after every KO. That turns an already respectable attacker into a genuine late-game cleaner that snowballs faster the more damage the [rest] of the team has already done. The [Ground]/[Dark] STAB combo is one of the cleanest in the format, with very few walls that resist both moves.

## Strengths
[moxie] creates something close to infinite setup — one KO and [earthquake] starts hitting like a Choice-locked Swords Dance hit. [knock-off] pulls triple duty: [Dark] STAB, 97 effective base power once the item is removed, and removal of leftovers or [focus-sash] from walls that would normally stop the [Ground] STAB. [stone-edge] keeps [Flying] and [Fire] pivots from coming in for free, and [close-combat] covers the [Steel] walls that sit comfortably on [earthquake] + [knock-off]. [moxie]'s snowball scales better than [intimidate]'s one-turn threat reduction once the game is past turn 8.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Fighting], [Grass], [Ice], [Water], and [Bug] all hit for 2x, which is a long list. Fast [Water]-[Fighting] attackers [revenge] [Krookodile] almost for free — a [sing]le [aqua-jet] or [mach-punch] priority hit wipes the snowball plan. [Fairy] walls resist [knock-off] and take neutral [earthquake] only, which is a dead matchup unless the team carries a [Steel] or [Poison] partner. [stealth-rock] takes a flat 12.5 percent every entry, which means [Krookodile] basically has to commit once and stay in — so hazard removal is non-negotiable.

## Recommended Moveset
[earthquake] is the locked STAB and the engine of the sweep. [knock-off] is the second mandatory slot and arguably the more broken move — it removes enemy items while delivering 97 effective base power. [stone-edge] is the [Rock] cove[rage] slot that punishes [Flying] or [Fire] switch-ins. [close-combat] is the fourth slot because [Krookodile] needs a way to break [Steel] walls and because it stacks [moxie] faster against [Normal] targets. [fire-fang] is the alternative for dedicated [Grass] matchups but is generally worse than [close-combat] in cove[rage].

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the best pick because [moxie] only works after the first KO — guaranteeing that KO with sash is the whole game plan. [choice-scarf] converts [Krookodile] into a [revenge] killer that outruns [tailwind] attackers and still keeps [moxie] stacks. [soft-sand] fits a sand-balanced team with constant [earthquake] spam. The ability has to be [moxie] — [intimidate] is nice on entry but the snowball from [moxie] scales harder as the game extends. Tera [Ground] stacks on top of [earthquake] for one devastating late-game c[lick]; Tera [Fairy] flips the [Fighting] and [Bug] weaknesses and also gives immunity to [Dragon] pivot [revenge]s.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Krookodile] needs softening work done before it enters — hazard setters, pivots that chip walls with [volt-switch] or [u-turn], and bulky partners that force trades all set up the snowball. A [Steel] or [Poison] partner is mandatory for [Fairy] matchups. Fast [Electric] or [Grass] answers (like a [Dragon] pivot) cover [Krookodile]'s [Water] and [Grass] weaknesses. A hazard remover behind [Krookodile] [protect]s its 12.5 percent [stealth-rock] vulnerability through multiple switches.

## Play Patterns
Do not lead [Krookodile] — save it for the mid-to-late game once the first one or two oppo[sing] mons are down. Target the weakest remaining opponent for the first KO to start [moxie] stacks, then [earthquake] through whatever is next. Avoid [close-combat] unless a [Steel] wall is the only thing left, because the defense drop amplifies priority [revenge] kills. Tera at the precise moment a [Fighting] or [Bug] hit would KO, not earlier — a surviving [moxie] stack ends the game.
